channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Mixed Integer Goal Programming for Personalized Meal Optimization with User-Defined Serving Granularity
item.description: arXiv:2605.13849v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Determining what to eat to satisfy nutritional requirements is one of the oldest optimization problems in operations research, yet existing formulations have two persistent limitations: continuous variables produce impractical fractional servings (1.7...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, paper, context, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Two-Dimensional Framework for AI Agent Design Patterns: Cognitive Function and Execution Topology
item.description: arXiv:2605.13850v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing frameworks for LLM-based agent architectures describe systems from a single perspective: industry guides (Anthropic, Google, LangChain) focus on execution topology -- how data flows -- while cognitive science surveys focus on cognitive functi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, LLM, framework, RAG, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Invisible Orchestrators Suppress Protective Behavior and Dissociate Power-Holders: Safety Risks in Multi-Agent LLM Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.13851v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent orchestration -- in which a hidden coordinator manages specialized worker agents -- is becoming the default architecture for enterprise AI deployment, yet the safety implications of orchestrator invisibility have never been empirically tes...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, LLM, alignment, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PREPING: Building Agent Memory without Tasks
item.description: arXiv:2605.13880v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agent memory is typically constructed either offline from curated demonstrations or online from post-deployment interactions. However, regardless of how it is built, an agent faces a cold-start gap when first introduced to a new environment without an...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, memory, experiment, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Conditional Attribute Estimation with Autoregressive Sequence Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.14004v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative models are often trained with a next-token prediction objective, yet many downstream applications require the ability to estimate or control sequence-level properties. Next-token prediction can lead to overfitting of local patterns during t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, transformer, arxiv, model, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Sheaf-Theoretic Transport and Obstruction for Detecting Scientific Theory Shift in AI Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.14033v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific theory shift in AI agents requires more than fitting equations to data. An artificial scientific agent must detect whether an existing representational framework remains transportable into a new regime, or whether its language has become lo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, context, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
